questions.question: What is the key difference between a while loop and a do-while loop in Java?
options: A while loop can iterate more times than a do-while loop
A do-while loop checks the condition after the body executes, guaranteeing at least one execution
A do-while loop does not require a boolean condition
A while loop always executes its body at least once
questions.answer: A do-while loop checks the condition after the body executes, guaranteeing at least one execution

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct syntax for a do-while loop in Java?
What happens if you omit the semicolon after the while condition in a do-while loop?
options: The loop runs normally but skips the first iteration
The loop becomes an infinite loop
The loop behaves like a regular while loop

questions.question: What is a sentinel value?
options: A variable that counts how many times a loop iterates
A special input value that signals the loop to stop
The initial value assigned to a loop counter
A boolean flag that starts as true and never changes
questions.answer: A special input value that signals the loop to stop

questions.question: When is a while loop generally preferred over a for loop?
options: When you know exactly how many iterations are needed
When you need the loop to run at least once
When you don't know in advance how many times the loop should run
When the loop body contains only one statement
questions.answer: When you don't know in advance how many times the loop should run

questions.question: Which loop type is most appropriate for input validation where the user must be prompted at least once?
What causes an infinite loop?
options: Using a do-while instead of a while loop
The loop condition never becomes false and no break statement is reached
Declaring the loop variable inside the loop body
questions.answer: The loop condition never becomes false and no break statement is reached

questions.question: Which of the following while loops will result in an infinite loop?
Why is it important that a sentinel value cannot be confused with valid data?
options: Because the sentinel must be a negative number
Because the compiler requires sentinel values to be constants
Because if valid data matches the sentinel, the loop will stop prematurely
Because sentinel values must always be of type String
questions.answer: Because if valid data matches the sentinel, the loop will stop prematurely

questions.question: Which method is used in the Day 18 material to make sentinel checking case-insensitive for String input?
What is the minimum number of times the body of a while loop can execute?
options: It depends on the data type of the condition

questions.question: What is the minimum number of times the body of a do-while loop can execute?
In an input validation pattern using a while loop, what must you do before the loop begins?
options: Initialize the variable to an invalid value so the loop condition is true on the first check
Declare the Scanner inside the loop
Set the loop counter to the maximum number of attempts
questions.answer: Initialize the variable to an invalid value so the loop condition is true on the first check

questions.question: Which of the following correctly uses a do-while loop for password validation with a maximum of 3 attempts?
Which of the following is a common prevention strategy for infinite loops?
options: Always use do-while loops instead of while loops
Ensure the loop body modifies a variable used in the condition so it eventually becomes false
Declare all loop variables as final
Always use a for loop instead of a while loop
questions.answer: Ensure the loop body modifies a variable used in the condition so it eventually becomes false

questions.question: In a sentinel-controlled loop that calculates the average of user-entered scores, when should the sentinel value be included in the average calculation?
options: Always, because it is part of the input
Only if the sentinel is a positive number
Never, because the sentinel signals termination and is not valid data
Only on the first iteration of the loop
questions.answer: Never, because the sentinel signals termination and is not valid data
